L1.area: Database connection and plugin initialization logic

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability allows an unauthenticated attacker to open and read arbitrary database files on the host system by supplying a crafted relative path with directory traversal sequences in a connection request. The user-controlled value is joined with a default directory without verifying that the resolved path remains within the intended directory, enabling access to any database file accessible to the application process.

L3.area: Database connection and plugin initialization logic
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability allows an unauthenticated attacker to open and read arbitrary database files on the host system by supplying a crafted relative path with directory traversal sequences in a connection request. The user-controlled value is joined with a default directory without verifying that the resolved path remains within the intended directory, enabling access to any database file accessible to the application process.

ground_truth.reason: The DB function joins a user-controlled database name with a default directory path without validating that the resolved path stays within the intended directory, allowing path traversal via sequences like '../' to access arbitrary Sqlite3 databases on the host.
